evals.expected_output: A complete pyramid-shaped test suite covering: (1) Unit tests with exhaustive Zod field validation (title min/max/missing, organizationId UUID format) and service branch tests with mocked Prisma (notFound branch, forbidden branch for each method); (2) Integration tests with real DB testing all service branches; (3) API tests for the POST /api/notes endpoint covering success, validation error, auth; (4) No E2E tests since there is no browser UI. The skill should explicitly select layers, read reference files, and produce properly structured test files.
expectations: The response selects Unit, Integration, and API layers and explicitly states E2E is skipped because no browser UI exists
Unit tests include exhaustive Zod field tests: title missing/empty/too-long (>255 chars)/valid-boundary (1 char, 255 chars), organizationId invalid UUID, organizationId missing

Unit tests for NotesService.getNote include: a test for the notFound branch (mock returns null → expect NotFoundError thrown) and a test for the forbidden branch (mock returns note with different userId → expect ForbiddenError thrown)
Unit tests use vi.mock for Prisma and verify mock was called with exact expected arguments
Unit tests create a unit factory file (e.g., notes.unit-factory.ts) for generating test data
Integration tests test service methods with real DB, not just HTTP endpoints
API tests cover at least: POST success (201), POST with missing title (400 with VALIDATION_ERROR), POST without auth (401)
All test files follow Arrange-Act-Assert pattern with clear sections
The response includes a file inventory listing all created test files per layer with run commands

evals.expected_output: API tests using Playwright as HTTP client. Tests should cover: success (200), unauthorized (401), forbidden/wrong user (403), not found (404), validation error (400). The skill should create test-owned payload interfaces (not import backend DTOs), a factory for generating user update data, and cleanup utilities. Tests should follow Arrange-Act-Assert with unique test data per test.
expectations: The response selects only the API layer and does NOT write unit or integration or E2E tests
API tests cover all 5 scenarios: 200 success, 401 unauthorized, 403 forbidden (different user), 404 not found, 400 validation error
Test file uses Playwright's APIRequestContext (e.g., `request` fixture), not fetch or axios
The test file creates a test-owned interface or type for the update payload — it does NOT import UpdateUserDto from the backend
Each test creates its own user (no shared state between tests) using a helper like authenticateUser()
Cleanup runs in afterEach to delete created users/data even when tests throw
Test data uses unique values (counter + timestamp or random) to avoid conflicts in parallel runs

evals.prompt: I added a login form to my Next.js app. The form has email and password fields and submits to /api/auth/login. After successful login the user is redirected to /dashboard. If credentials are wrong it shows an error message 'Invalid email or password'. The form has data-testid='login-form', data-testid='email-input', data-testid='password-input', data-testid='submit-button', data-testid='error-message'. Write E2E tests for the login flow.
evals.expected_output: E2E tests using Playwright with chromium project. Tests should cover: successful login → redirect to /dashboard, failed login → error message displayed, empty form submission → validation. The skill should create a Page Object Model for the login page using data-testid selectors, use API-based setup (not UI-based) to create users, inject auth cookies where needed, and avoid any static waits. No unit/integration/API tests since the ask is specifically E2E.
expectations: The response selects only the E2E layer and does NOT write unit, integration, or API tests
A Page Object Model (POM) class is created for the login page with methods and locators using data-testid selectors only (no CSS class selectors or XPath)
The POM class has at least: a locator for the form, email input, password input, submit button, and error message
Test for successful login: creates user via API (not via UI), navigates to login page, fills form, submits, asserts redirect to /dashboard using page.waitForURL() or expect(page).toHaveURL()
Test for failed login: attempts login with wrong credentials, asserts error message is visible using locator assertion (not page.waitForTimeout)
Test data uses unique values per test (counter/timestamp pattern) — no hardcoded test@example.com
Cleanup of created test users runs in afterEach
